---
title: "Project2"
author: "Arezoo G"
date: "2026-05-25"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

---
title: "Project II: Multiple Regression Modeling"
subtitle: "King County House Sales"
author: "Your Name(s)"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true
    fig_caption: true
  geometry: margin=1in
  fontsize: 11pt
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 6.8,
  fig.height = 4.8,
  out.width = "85%"
)

required_packages <- c(
  "readr", "ggplot2", "broom", "lmtest",
  "knitr", "dplyr", "geosphere"
)
missing_packages <- required_packages[
  !vapply(required_packages, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)
]

#install.packages("sp")
#install.packages("geosphere")  # if you haven’t already

library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)
library(lmtest)
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
library(geosphere)

format_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(p), NA_character_,
    ifelse(p < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.4f", p))
  )
}

theme_set(
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
)
```

# Summary of Data

```{r load-data}
house_df <- read_csv(
  "/Users/arezoog/Desktop/Spring 2026/STA135/Data/kc_house_data.csv"
)
```

```{r data-summary-table}
summary_vars <- c(
  "price", "bedrooms", "bathrooms", "sqft_living",
  "sqft_lot", "floors", "waterfront", "view",
  "condition", "grade", "yr_built", "yr_renovated"
)

summary_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = summary_vars,
  Mean = sapply(house_df[, summary_vars], mean, na.rm = TRUE),
  SD = sapply(house_df[, summary_vars], sd, na.rm = TRUE),
  Minimum = sapply(house_df[, summary_vars], min, na.rm = TRUE),
  Maximum = sapply(house_df[, summary_vars], max, na.rm = TRUE)
)

summary_df[, -1] <- round(summary_df[, -1], 2)

kable(
  summary_df,
  caption = "Summary statistics for the key variables in the King County dataset",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

# Explanatory Plot

```{r price-histogram, fig.cap="Distribution of home sale prices in King County (May 2014 -- May 2015)"}
ggplot(house_df, aes(x = price)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60, fill = "#1B4F72", color = "white", alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_dollar(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "House Price Distribution",
    subtitle = "Prices are strongly right-skewed",
    x = "Sale price",
    y = "Count"
  )
```



```{r bathroom-boxplot, fig.cap="Sale price by number of bathrooms"}
bath_df <- house_df %>%
  mutate(bathrooms_f = factor(round(bathrooms * 2) / 2))

ggplot(bath_df, aes(x = bathrooms_f, y = price)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#1B4F72", alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_dollar(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "House Price versus Bathroom Count",
    x = "Number of bathrooms",
    y = "Sale price"
  )
```

```{r sqft-lot-scatter, fig.cap="Sale price versus total lot square footage"}
ggplot(house_df, aes(x = sqft_lot, y = price)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.3, color = "#1B4F72") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_dollar(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Price vs. Total Lot Square Footage",
    subtitle = "No clear linear relationship; lot size is heavily right-skewed",
    x = "Lot square footage",
    y = "Sale price"
  )
```

```{r grade-boxplot, fig.cap="Sale price by construction grade"}
ggplot(house_df, aes(x = factor(grade), y = price)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#1B4F72", alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_dollar(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "House Price versus Construction Grade",
    subtitle = "Price increases with grade; relationship is non-linear",
    x = "Grade",
    y = "Sale price"
  )
```

```{r waterfront-view-scatter, fig.cap="Sale price versus view score, colored by waterfront status"}
wf_df <- house_df %>%
  mutate(
    Waterfront = ifelse(
      !is.na(waterfront) & waterfront == 1,
      "Waterfront",
      "Non-Waterfront"
    )
  )

ggplot(wf_df, aes(x = view, y = price, color = Waterfront)) +
  geom_jitter(size = 1.0, alpha = 0.5, width = 0.15) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Non-Waterfront" = "#555555", "Waterfront" = "#1B4F72")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_dollar(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "View Score vs. Price by Waterfront Status",
    subtitle = "Waterfront homes command higher prices at every view level",
    x = "View score",
    y = "Sale price",
    color = NULL
  )
```

```{r sqft-living-scatter, fig.cap="Sale price versus interior living square footage"}
ggplot(house_df, aes(x = sqft_living, y = price)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.3, color = "#1B4F72") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_dollar(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Price vs. Living Square Footage",
    subtitle = "Clear positive association; spread increases above 5,000 sq ft",
    x = "Living square footage",
    y = "Sale price"
  )
```


# Data Cleaning and Distance Feature

```{r data-cleaning-and-distance}
df <- as.data.frame(house_df)

df$sqft_basement <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(df$sqft_basement)))
df$sqft_basement[is.na(df$sqft_basement)] <- 0

df$yr_renovated[is.na(df$yr_renovated)] <- 0
df$waterfront[is.na(df$waterfront)] <- 0

df <- df[!is.na(df$view), ]

house_coords <- cbind(df$long, df$lat)
downtown_coord <- c(-122.334862, 47.604942)

df$ft_to_downtown <- distHaversine(house_coords, downtown_coord) * 3.28084

drop_cols <- c("id", "date", "sqft_living15", "sqft_lot15", "zipcode", "lat", "long")
house_df_clean <- df[, !(colnames(df) %in% drop_cols)]
```


# Model Fitting

```{r full-and-final-models}
# Full model
full_model <- lm(
  price ~ bedrooms + bathrooms + sqft_living + sqft_lot + floors +
    waterfront + view + condition + grade + sqft_above +
    sqft_basement + yr_built + yr_renovated + ft_to_downtown,
  data = house_df_clean
)

# Remove identified outliers by index (from diagnostics)
outlier_rows <- c(15468, 3833, 7082)
house_df_final <- house_df_clean[-outlier_rows, ]

# Final selected model (no sqft_above, sqft_basement, distance)
final_model <- lm(
  price ~ bedrooms + bathrooms + sqft_living + sqft_lot + floors +
    waterfront + view + condition + grade + yr_built + yr_renovated,
  data = house_df_final
)
```

```{r model-fit-values}
model_summary <- summary(final_model)
final_tidy <- tidy(final_model, conf.int = TRUE)
final_glance <- glance(final_model)

r_sq <- final_glance$r.squared
adj_r <- final_glance$adj.r.squared
f_stat <- final_glance$statistic
f_df1 <- final_glance$df
f_df2 <- final_glance$df.residual
```


# Analysis


```{r coef-table}
coef_table <- final_tidy[, c(
  "term", "estimate", "std.error",
  "statistic", "p.value", "conf.low", "conf.high"
)]
names(coef_table) <- c(
  "Term", "Estimate", "Std. Error",
  "t statistic", "p-value", "95% CI lower", "95% CI upper"
)

coef_table[, c(
  "Estimate", "Std. Error", "t statistic",
  "95% CI lower", "95% CI upper"
)] <- round(
  coef_table[, c(
    "Estimate", "Std. Error", "t statistic",
    "95% CI lower", "95% CI upper"
  )],
  2
)

coef_table$`p-value` <- format_p(as.numeric(final_tidy$p.value))

kable(
  coef_table,
  caption = "Estimated coefficients for the final model",
  align = c("l", rep("r", 6))
)
```

```{r living-vs-lot}
model_living <- lm(
  price ~ bedrooms + bathrooms + sqft_living + floors +
    waterfront + view + condition + grade + yr_built + yr_renovated,
  data = house_df_final
)

model_lot <- lm(
  price ~ bedrooms + bathrooms + sqft_lot + floors +
    waterfront + view + condition + grade + yr_built + yr_renovated,
  data = house_df_final
)

adj_r2_living <- round(summary(model_living)$adj.r.squared, 4)
adj_r2_lot <- round(summary(model_lot)$adj.r.squared, 4)

t_living <- round(coef(model_summary)["sqft_living", "t value"], 2)
t_lot <- round(coef(model_summary)["sqft_lot", "t value"], 2)
```

```{r grade-partial-f}
model_no_grade <- lm(
  price ~ bedrooms + bathrooms + sqft_living + sqft_lot + floors +
    waterfront + view + condition + yr_built + yr_renovated,
  data = house_df_final
)

pf_grade <- anova(model_no_grade, final_model)
grade_f <- round(pf_grade[2, "F"], 2)
grade_df1 <- pf_grade[2, "Df"]
grade_df2 <- pf_grade[2, "Res.Df"]
grade_p_fmt <- format_p(pf_grade[2, "Pr(>F)"])

kable(
  pf_grade, digits = 4,
  caption = "Partial F-test: reduced model without grade versus full model"
)
```

```{r interaction-test}
model_interaction <- lm(
  price ~ bedrooms + bathrooms + sqft_living + sqft_lot + floors +
    waterfront * view + condition + grade + yr_built + yr_renovated,
  data = house_df_final
)

pf_interact <- anova(final_model, model_interaction)
int_f <- round(pf_interact[2, "F"], 2)
int_df1 <- pf_interact[2, "Df"]
int_df2 <- pf_interact[2, "Res.Df"]
int_p_fmt <- format_p(pf_interact[2, "Pr(>F)"])

interact_ci <- confint(model_interaction)["waterfront:view", ]
int_est <- round(
  coef(summary(model_interaction))["waterfront:view", "Estimate"],
  2
)
int_ci_lo <- round(interact_ci, 2)[1]
int_ci_hi <- round(interact_ci, 2)[2]

kable(
  pf_interact, digits = 4,
  caption = "Partial F-test: additive versus waterfront x view interaction model"
)
```


# Diagnostics

```{r diagnostics}
diag_data <- augment(final_model)
diag_data$sqrt_abs_std_resid <- sqrt(abs(diag_data$.std.resid))
diag_data$index <- seq_len(nrow(diag_data))

leverage_cutoff <- 2 * length(coef(final_model)) / nrow(diag_data)
cook_cutoff <- 4 / nrow(diag_data)
top_influence <- diag_data[
  order(diag_data$.cooksd, decreasing = TRUE)[1:3],
]
```

```{r diag-plots, fig.height=6, fig.width=8}
plot_2x2 <- function(p1, p2, p3, p4) {
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::pushViewport(grid::viewport(layout = grid::grid.layout(2, 2)))
  print(p1, vp = grid::viewport(layout.pos.row = 1, layout.pos.col = 1))
  print(p2, vp = grid::viewport(layout.pos.row = 1, layout.pos.col = 2))
  print(p3, vp = grid::viewport(layout.pos.row = 2, layout.pos.col = 1))
  print(p4, vp = grid::viewport(layout.pos.row = 2, layout.pos.col = 2))
}

p_resid_fit <- ggplot(diag_data, aes(x = .fitted, y = .resid)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  geom_point(size = 1.7, alpha = 0.8, color = "#1B4F72") +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess", formula = y ~ x,
    se = FALSE, color = "#D55E00", linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted", x = "Fitted values", y = "Residuals")

p_qq <- ggplot(diag_data, aes(sample = .std.resid)) +
  stat_qq(size = 1.7, alpha = 0.8, color = "#1B4F72") +
  stat_qq_line(color = "#D55E00", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Normal Q-Q",
    x = "Theoretical quantiles", y = "Standardized residuals"
  )

p_scale <- ggplot(diag_data, aes(x = .fitted, y = sqrt_abs_std_resid)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.7, alpha = 0.8, color = "#1B4F72") +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess", formula = y ~ x,
    se = FALSE, color = "#D55E00", linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Scale-Location", x = "Fitted values",
    y = expression(sqrt(abs("standardized residual")))
  )

p_leverage <- ggplot(diag_data, aes(x = .hat, y = .std.resid)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-2, 2), linetype = "dotted", color = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = leverage_cutoff, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.85, color = "#1B4F72") +
  geom_text(
    data = top_influence, aes(label = index),
    nudge_y = 0.25, size = 2.8, show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Residuals vs Leverage",
    x = "Leverage", y = "Standardized residuals"
  )

plot_2x2(p_resid_fit, p_qq, p_scale, p_leverage)
```

```{r cooks-distance-plot}
ggplot(diag_data, aes(x = index, y = .cooksd)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(xend = index, y = 0, yend = .cooksd),
    linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 0.75, color = "#1B4F72"
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 1.9, alpha = 0.9, color = "#1B4F72") +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = cook_cutoff, linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#C0392B", linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = top_influence, aes(label = index),
    nudge_y = 0.015, size = 2.8, show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Cook's Distance",
    subtitle = "Dashed line marks the 4/n screening rule",
    x = "Observation",
    y = "Cook's distance"
  )
```

```{r assumption-tests}
set.seed(123)

shapiro_result <- shapiro.test(sample(residuals(final_model), 5000))
bp_result <- lmtest::bptest(final_model)
bp_stat <- as.numeric(bp_result$statistic)
bp_p <- bp_result$p.value

dw_result <- lmtest::dwtest(final_model)
dw_stat <- as.numeric(dw_result$statistic)
dw_p <- dw_result$p.value

diagnostic_table <- data.frame(
  Assumption = c("Normality", "Constant Variance", "Independence"),
  Test = c("Shapiro-Wilk", "Breusch-Pagan", "Durbin-Watson"),
  Statistic = round(c(
    as.numeric(shapiro_result$statistic),
    bp_stat,
    dw_stat
  ), 4),
  `p-value` = c(
    shapiro_result$p.value,
    bp_p,
    dw_p
  ),
  check.names = FALSE
)

kable(diagnostic_table, caption = "Formal Assumption Tests")
```
